You've got a lot to learn afore you can join a finship crew.
Finship? What finship?
Thank you for dinner, Javi. It was delicious.
Señora Juana... I have some questions.
Go on then, no need to hold back.
I feel it's a little... risky, to be hunting such a massive saltfin with the men we have.
Wouldn't it be safer to go after a school of smaller ones?
The saltfin schools we've reeled in have been far too small. We'll need a lot more alchemical materials for what is to come.
We've already prepared plenty.
Still far from enough. The compass was just the beginning. We'll need so much more once we enter the ocean.
Poz, the last cook, said you like to make some crazy decisions. Most of the time, it makes our blood run hot with excitement.
But other times, it makes our blood turn to ice.
...Yet he was ever-loyal to me, supporting even those decisions that made him freeze up inside.
Is that what you expect from me too?
No. Which is why he's the previous cook and you're the new one.
Do you really wish to spend the rest of your life worrying about your next meal? This is our chance. Seize it, and all our problems vanish forever.
The ocean managed to nurture the entirety of the Golden Age, but our entire fleet can't even compare to your average gang of smugglers.
But the Salt Flat has nurtured us for decades! It hasn't been easy, but you've led us through the years.
The Flat is too barren for us.
And the ocean is too dangerous for us.
Think about the townsfolk. They would like nothing more than to be rid of us, and their net draws ever closer.
You were never afraid of them!
It's not them that I'm afraid of.
It's the Flat. Its ecosystem is about to collapse.
That fissure was just the beginning. Survey teams are reporting that large voids have appeared beneath the Salt Flat, where underground rivers have dried up.
Voids?
The underground rivers are drying up. The number of microorganisms has dropped sharply, and with it, the number of saltfins that feed upon them.
It's been a long time since one of our finships encountered a large school of saltfins.
Why didn't you tell us?
What was I supposed to say? That we're finished if we don't escape this place? What would that accomplish other than causing a panic?
Is this the only way then?
...Yes, it's the only way.
A little to the left... thanks.
Mmph!
Yes... now a little harder.
Mmph—!
That was a long day, learning to catch saltfins with Señor Rus... Ow, my aching shoulders...
Mm, yes, that's the spot.
Nene stomps even harder on Isidro's back as he inhales and exhales deeply.
Nene...
Mm-ph!
Never mind, you wouldn't understand.
...It's not like I don't want to help you all.
When I was little, a beggar taught me a few tricks, just to earn scraps on the streets.
But when I met my mentor, he told me those weren't tricks.
It was alchemy.
Mm... hmph...
I was fascinated by alchemical studies. It earned me praise from my mentor, and recognition from my peers.
And most importantly, I was no longer a street urchin with nothing to my name.
I was worth something.
That is, until I learned the most fundamental nature of that school of alchemy...
Isidro pauses. Nene stops stomping and looks up perplexedly.

lines.st: You can never abandon what you have, Isidro.
You fear you will go back to having nothing if you fail.
But the nature of alchemy is to achieve your goal, devoting everything to it, no matter the cost.
The stakes, everything... The return, a chance.
Why is it that only our alchemy was used to sail the seas? Because the ocean demands such quality.

lines.x: Sensing Isidro's depression, Nene lies down on his back and chirps gently.
Rus was right. You all just want to live a decent life. Repairing the compass and going to sea... It's not worth the risk.
But I'm curious... how exactly is Juana's compass constructed?
The Corazonix inside... does it truly point towards whatever your heart yearns for the most, as the stories say?
If I just had the chance to study it...
Did you fall asleep?
I'm curious. What's going on in my own heart?
Flowers?
Doctor Añora was the first to plant them in town, decades ago. For use in ointments.
Ah... I have seen them before, in the halls of the Bastión de Cánticos.
I've never been.
All I know is that it is the first flower to bloom from the salted earth of Aarón, after so many years of being besieged by the sea.
The townsfolk used to give me these, as a token of appreciation I think.
Now, I feel I should be offering them to you.
No, señor, I am unworthy.
Had you not taken those... measures towards the caravans, to obtain the predator burdenbeasts...
We could never have gone after those despicable bandits in the Salt Flat.
I did no more than what must be done.
Your duty, of course.
I believe you mentioned you are after a forbidden relic the bandits have stolen.
What sort of relic could it possibly be, to bring you all the way to this barren land?
A... compass.
There are thousands of antique compasses from old Iberia.
None like this. This one corrupts the heart.
It points towards the user's deepest desires, dredged from the depths of the heart... Or so it is said.
But those who cast their eyes towards such base desires can never turn back.
It drives them to theft, robbery, murder. They cannot stop themselves, try as they might.
...You really think so?
It is simply the truth.
Is this what you've been preaching to the townsfolk these past few days?
It was not I who preached. The people here already understood this truth, more profoundly than I ever have.
Someone had already revealed it to them. Perhaps you?
...Yes, it was me.
It was I who issued those prohibitions.
Why stop then? Why lift the prohibition?
Look at this town. No one here dares touch the new goods and technologies that the caravans bring. No one dares leave their house after dark.
They dare not fill their stomachs... even in years of plenty.
Every word I used to shackle these poor people has continued to shackle them.
They see it differently.
Was it not your deeds that helped them lay aside their dreams and laments for wealth and power. Was it not your words that encouraged them to take up the hoe and the scythe?
Times have changed, Señor Alguacil.
Times never change, Señor Calificador.
The deepest desires of the heart should never be probed. Not by anyone, not anywhere.
Never.
Saltfins ahoy!
Two meters wide, eighteen long!
Now that's a big one. Chase 'em down!
Full speed ahead! Harpoons ready!
Hard to port! Full speed!
Harpoons up! Hey, ho!♪
In the waves the big one rolls!♪
Stomp the planks! Hoist the sails!♪
Drown the high wind's wail!♪
Hey, ho! Up we go!♪
Before the sun sinks low!♪
Wha'cha watching from a corner for, chaval?
Oh, nothing...
Ugh!
Watch yourself. But the shaking is a good thing. Means we're getting close.
Where'd the boss put you? Reeling in the net from here, or joining the hunt in the salts?
Señora Juana won't let me leave the ship.
I don't get it. With your swordhand, you'd be way more useful in the hunt.
She didn't give me any particular job either. Just told me to do whatever I can.
Weird. She's usually so detailed.
Maybe the standards are laxer for the new guy?
I see it!
Finshit... that's a BIG one...
Lower the boat! Don't let it get away!
Wait, it's turning!
Full rudder! It's running into us!
Not in the best mood, big guy? Well, my mood couldn't be better.
Change of plans, Perlita. Javier stays aboard. I'll lead the hunt myself.
But señora, you haven't led a hunt in ages.
Do as I say!
Javier! Come back! Take the helm!
It's too dangerous, señora!
This one's too much for you, Javi. Stay on the ship. I need you to work with me.
Then what should I do?
See that salt reef in the shape of a tongue? Herd it there with salt rounds. I'll handle the rest.
Aye aye.
Full rudder! Hang tight!
The salt ship makes a hard turn to starboard, but not quite quickly enough to avoid brushing against the side of the charging saltfin.
Cold sweat rolls down everyone's backs at the grating sound. Hanging onto a fence for dear life, Isidro looks towards where the sound came from.
He sees a massive saltfin swim past the ship, sending stinging salt grains flying across the deck.
How could a saltfin grow this big?
I thought that last one was the biggest I'd ever see...
No, chaval... Even I've never seen one this big.
What?
We never hunt ones this big, not by choice.
This is loco! We're in way over our heads!
Load! Aim! Fire!
Port broadside! Fire!
Aim for the eyes and nostril! Hitting the shell won't do a damn thing!
Is Señora Juana in position?
Boss! Boss!
She just went down!
You gotta stop the hunt! We're in way over our heads!
And how would we eat tomorrow if we don't hunt today?
What's the matter, Rus?
It's twice as big as any other saltfin. Only the leader of a school could grow this big.
So you'd expect it to have a whole school behind it, but I sure don't see one.
Get to the point, professor.
Either it lost its school, or it was abandoned. Either way, it's mad. Real mad.
Does Señora Juana know about this?
It was her plan.
She's toying with our lives again. If that thing decides to bring us down with it, we're all—
Mm...
Teach...
Señora and the hunting team have already left. It's too late to back down.
Don't let the rest of the crew know. We can't afford a panic.
Keep firing, Javier.
I'd rather die on a hunt today than of starvation in a month.
Boss... Can we really take on this big guy?
Of course. And we'll win.
Wait for it to turn and aim for gaps in its shell! If you can hit the nostril, all the better!
It's coming straight for us!
Row! Hard forward!
Aye aye!
Harpoons ready! ...Now!
Argh!
Good! Keep it up!
It's too strong! I can't hold this rope much longer!
I'm with you!
All hands! Again!
Argh!
Good! Someone hit the nostril!
Hold that rope like your life depends on it!
Hah!
Thirty meters left!
Keep holding on!
Twenty meters!
Ten meters!
We're out of rope!
Just a little more!
It's coming out of the salt layer!
Now! Reel it in!
Juana rides her boat towards the saltfin as the rope is reeled in. Its thick hide has been pierced by dozens of harpoons, blood seeping out from the wounds.
Listening to its heavy, rapid breathing, Juana knows that its life is coming to an end.

She cannot help but place her hand on the massive figure, feeling its life flow out of it little by little, until the saltfin closes its eyes, still raging against the dying of the light with its last breath.
Phew... finally.
We did it!
...Hail the salt ship. Have them come for us.
Aye aye!
Wait!
Look out, señora!
It's not dead yet...
Juana feels a gaze filled with overwhelming malice upon her.
She looks up at its eyes, filled with hatred and anger.
The next instant, the saltfin begins flailing violently, sending blood-stained salt flying, falling over the sailors like rain.
It breaks free of the ropes, leaving behind only the harpoons still impaled in its hide.
It raises its tail fin high.
Retreat! Retreat!
No...
Several boats are quickly snapped in two by the saltfin's attack, and the air is filled with the screams of injured sailors.
The sound seems to fill Juana with a certain resolve. She charges towards the rampaging saltfin, and grabs one of the harpoons on its back.
It leaps up, trying to shake her off.
It's too dangerous, señora! Let go!
No! This one is mine, as long as I still breathe!
The saltfin sweeps aside the boats in front of it and swims into the distance, carrying Juana on its back.
It does so with astounding speed, like an arrow from a bow, or a dying star.
Faster!
We have to catch it!
Stop! Stop! That's as far as we can go! The beast dragged Juana into the Skeletal Reef!
Javier... Javier...
You were right, Rus. I shouldn't have knocked you out.
Not the first time. I know how you can get.
But listen for once... We can't chase her. The steam in Skeletal Reef will tear our ship apart.
If you won't go, give me a boat. I'll find her.
No one survives that place! Not us, not...
Juana can.
Are you with me or not, Teach?
Don't be reckless, Javier!
You're just gonna let her die?
No one wants to lose our leader. But we can't lose you too.
She liked you. If something happens to her, you have a responsibility for what comes next.
I'll go.
Maybe so, Teach. But she won't die. I won't let her!
Who's with me?!
Count me in!
And me!
Would you mind if I join you?
The sailors part to let Isidro through.
If it isn't our resident alchemist. I figured you'd keep your mouth shut forever.
What could you possibly do, chaval?
I can help you get out of that valley alive.
We're ready, Señor Silver.
Good. Take them in at my command.
Yes, sir!
...Are you sure those bandits will pass through here, Señor Alguacil?
Yes... they're short on supplies.
Saltfins are their only food source in the Salt Flat. From what the caravans tell us, they must travel through this passage when they return from a hunt.
Exhausted by their quarry, they will be no match for us.
Good.
With this ambush, it will all be over.
Things will improve in town once we have the bandits' technology and supplies.
Right, Anastasio?
Anastasio mutters something, but Silver does not hear it.
He simply gazes upon the vast Salt Flat before him, as if seeing the town's prosperous future out there.
Watch out! Steam pillar.
So this is your brilliant plan? To have us all die here?
Sorry... I'll be more careful.
The test tube that Isidro throws bursts in mid-air, creating a golden mist.
The mist is soon dispersed by a faintly red jet of air.
Followed by a powerful burst of steam.
This will let us predict the steam bursts.
But I don't have too many left. We'll need to hurry.
Hmph...
Wreckage ahead, Javier. Watch out.
I could blow it up.
No need. I know what you can do, chaval.
Okay... this ship looks like it's been here for a while.
It's the first salt ship Juana ever built. She thought it could take us through Skeletal Reef and back to the sea.
She failed.
And lost almost everything.
You see any trace of the boss?
Yes. Not far now.
The ship continues sailing, past a row of towering bone spikes. The cracks running through the surface are filled with blood.
Saltfin blood. Don't let it get to you.
Isidro picks up a piece of salt that rolled down from the cliffs and throws it out.
He opens his palm, and finds it sticky with the blood from the salt.
There's blood everywhere. What kind of fight happened here?
Is the boss even alive?
We'll find out soon.
Past the bone pillars, a massive saltfin lies lifeless on the ground, its skull punctured by a helm wheel.
The master of the wheel lays still on its back, blood dripping down her feet.
Señora...
Juana... no...
They disembark and climb the beast's back. Javier touches the woman's arm. It is still warm, but that fact fills him with even more sorrow.
I'm sorry. We were too late.
*Cough* *Cough*... Yes, you were.
S-Señora?!
And there I was, hoping I wouldn't need to do all the work myself.
Are you okay, boss? You're covered in blood.
It's not mine. It put up a good fight, but I outlasted it.
You got anything to drink, Isidro?
Of course.
Juana takes the bottle, climbs down the saltfin's back, and sits down, leaning against it.
She takes a few swigs, and looks at the crowd that had gathered with a smile on her face.
Saltfin garum... What a shame. What I wouldn't do for some fine rum right about now.
Could you lend me a shoulder, Isidro? I have to admit I'm rather spent after all that.
Me?
I'll do it.
Let the boy do it. You folks need to start butchering this saltfin.
And take that wheel out for me, won't you, Javi?
Sí, señora.
Over here, boy.
A-Alright.
Now, now, you never rush a lady. Always let her finish her business.
Juana unhurriedly downs the liquid in the bottle, casually tossing the bottlecap into the air and catching it with her other hand. Isidro can only watch and wait.
There is movement in Juana's throat from time to time. She takes her time swallowing.
Or, rather, it takes great effort for her to swallow.
(Whisper) Why are you hiding your injury?
(Whisper) Just hold on to me, steady now...
(Whisper) Argh...
(Whisper) Listen, joven. I can't get hurt.
(Whisper) Fleet morale has been shaky enough already... I have to seem invincible.
(Whisper) You just gave them an entire month's worth of meals.
(Whisper) They'd take care of you.
(Whisper) I'm not worried about betrayal, but... if I go down, they'll think the situation is dire, and that's when they start panicking.
(Whisper) So, keep me steady... I don't want to fall on my face in front of everyone.
(Whisper) Argh...
(Whisper) Your ribs are cracked. I can't hold you too hard.
(Whisper) It's alright... I can take it.
Señor Calificador, there is a salt ship approaching from the southwest.
Hand me your scope.
Seems like they've had quite the catch. Just look at that thing they're dragging behind...
Would you like to see for yourself?
No need.
...I'll give the order to attack.
Silver raises the spyglass. Through the round lens, he sees a skull flag flapping in the wind.
In all his years on the Salt Flat, he had never looked forward to it so much.
How is it?
Enough for everyone, and then some.
Good. Then we'll have plenty of materials to repair the compass.
...I guess.
Señora... I...
Yes?
Leave the rest to me. You're tired; you need rest.
...I suppose you're right. I know I can count on you.
Need any help getting back to your quarters?
Just do your job. I'm exhausted, not crippled.
...Aye aye.
Got a question, chaval.
Yeah?
Is the boss hurt?
Why would you think that? There's nothing wrong with her.
Don't lie to me. Liars get lashes in this crew, you know.
Of course.
*Sigh*...
Are you worried?
Of course I am. She never shows weakness in front of the others. I hardly ever see her get injured... but for some reason she keeps getting new scars.
If she's hurt, I don't want her to hide it from us.
...She'll be fine.
Target in range!
Fire at will.
Argh—!
What was that?!
It's the Inquisition!
Where's my wheel?
They came for our quarry.
But you're... I'll go! Just wait inside!
And watch as the Inquisition takes what's mine? Never.
Juana!
Juana!!
Promise me you'll—
Look out!
What the—
Argh—!
Ambush! Take cover!
Mierda, it's the Inquisition!
What's going on?
Wish I knew. Someone must've leaked our hunting route.
Teach! Those finshits came for our catch!
Tell me something I don't know! What about the guards?
They were caught by surprised. We've got casualties.
Tell them to pull back!
What about the saltfin?
Is that meat more important than our lives?
Full speed ahead! Shake them!
I'm not part of your crew, though. I'll check on the saltfin.
Argh—
The guards around the saltfin have been neutralized. We cut the rope. They can't pull the meat with them.
And the remaining pirates?
Hiding inside the ship.
A shame... We could have taken the ship too if we had more men.
What of the Alguacil?
Went to pursue the ones who fled.
Tell him to return. We have the saltfin. No need to overextend ourselves.
Señor!
A massive helm wheel flies towards Silver. He jumps back in the nick of time and almost falls backwards.
A woman in black leaps down from the salt ship, catching the flying helm wheel in her hand.
Where do you think you're going with my catch, Inquisitor?
You... The corrupting Ægir.
Oh, it's you, joven. I thought you might grow up a little when you became calificador, but it seems you're no different from your treacherous predecessor, Silver.
Always looking to stab people in the back.
Did you expect to be treated kindly, sinner?
Was I being too nice? Would you prefer to continue this conversation with the sword?
Did you perhaps think that by killing the arbiter of justice you would become one?
I was wondering what got you bold enough to venture into the Salt Flat. You found a backer.
That woman is the leader of the pirates, Señor Alguacil!
Then you're the mastermind behind the theft of the compass... Tell me, do you cling to it even now?
And what loathsome desires has it drawn out from you?
(Damn it, my wounds... I can't dodge!)
Are you out of your mind? He almost hit you.
(Whisper) Silence, joven. Do you think I wanted to get hit?
We need to get back to the ship. That guy's too strong for us. We can't fight him like this.
Ah, we meet again.
Then you shall receive judgment together.
We need to go! They came prepared, while we're exhausted!
If you're not going to help, then stay out of my way!
Teach just gave the order to sail! You'll be left behind!
The ship needs to shake them! We can't wait for you!
You go back.
Juana tries to stand, but cannot find the strength.
The Alguacil and warrior priests approach. In the distance, Silver stands atop her catch with a smug expression.
Enough food to feed the fleet for a month. Important materials for repairing the compass.
She cannot afford to lose it, if she is to return to the ocean.
'Señora Juana! Come back!'
Juana hears Javier's voice calling to her from the ship's stern.
Grab Juana, chaval! We're throwing the ladder down!
Blood... your wounds have opened up!
What're you waiting for?! Get up here!
She must not pass out. Her crew needs her. There is a future she must lead her fleet into.
She made a promise to Cruz.
Don't let them get away!
Yes, sir!
Señora Juana!
Juana!
Señora Juana!
Shut... up...
She can withstand the pain and darkness back no longer. They overcome her.
Ugh...
Juana! You're awake.
Don't worry, we're safe.
Argh... where's Teach?
She's busy elsewhere. I'm here by your side.
What about the boy... Where did he go? Did he run away...?
No, he brought you back.
Do you want to see him? I can go get him.
What is he doing?
...He's with Teach, treating the crew members who were injured in the attack.
Aaaahhh— argh—!
I'm sorry, this ointment hurts when it's applied, but the bleeding should stop soon.
Now shut up and stop whining!
Go on... argh... I can... take it...
How's it going on your end?
(Shakes head) Not much we can do...
Bury the dead in the salts as soon as we can.
Aye... Come with me, chaval. Cesc wants to see you.
Is he hurt?
Got shot in the liver. He's on his last legs... said he wanted to see you.
...Take me to him.
Isidro makes his way through the wounded, a bowl of hot soup in hand.
Amongst the many pale faces, he sees a familiar one, smiling at him with what little strength it has left.
He sits beside the wounded man and smiles back.
You're damn ugly when you smile... you know that?
Sounds like you're in good spirits.
Here, I brought you some soup. You'll feel better.
No... don't waste it.
Thanks for coming to see me... and for saving my hide back then.
I wanted to ask... why did you save me...?
I've thought I was going to die so many time over my journeys.
But every time, I was lucky enough to meet someone who cared. I know how it feels to be waiting for death.
It's not pleasant.
I... *cough* I never knew a damn thing about you... Tell me more...
You got... a home?
No, I don't.
A family?
Not any I grew up with. My parents left when I was very young.
...I see.
How 'bout you tell him where you learned your bag of tricks?
Sure...
Some different people took pity on me and taught me how to survive. Bits and pieces, here and there.
There was a teacher who taught me how to use a sword.
We lived together for a long time. I learned a lot from him.
Must've invested a lot in you.
Yeah. He raised me as his successor.
Why didn't... you stay... with him...
I couldn't live that way. He made me feel safe.
What's... wrong with that?
Nothing's worse than feeling safe. It makes you let your guard down. But nowhere is truly safe.
You know, amigo...
You don't have a home... don't like feeling safe, don't want to settle down... living side-by-side with danger...
You were... born to be a pirate...
Maybe I was.
Isidro says no more. The soup has long since become cold in the night wind of the Salt Flat.
He raises the bowl and takes a sip. It has been a month, and he is already used to its thick, salty flavor.
G-Gracias...
I injured a holy man when I corralled the burdenbeasts and startled one by accident. I will forsake half of my serving as penance.
No, eat up so that it doesn't happen again.
There's enough saltfin for everyone. Eat.
But, Señor Silver, overeating is—
Is better than letting food go to waste. Saltfin flesh won't last in this weather. Finish it before it spoils.
Kindly bring these to our injured brothers, my good chef.
They need something nutritious to eat, after the beating they've taken these past few days.
Have you reviewed the plan, Señor Alguacil?
I do not understand how such a notion could have even occurred to you.
If the bandits surrender the compass and the blueprints for a salt ship, I will pardon most of them and permit them to live as I allow them to.
Many are young and healthy. They will be invaluable labor if this town is to move.
Besides, it will convenient to have sword hands unbound by religious dogma, in the event of any future negotiations.
They will destroy your town.
Eliminate the Ægir leading them, and the rest are just a disorganized mob. The three whom we have locked up in the basement are each playing their own game.
I will split them and give them conflicting interests, such that if they do raise their swords, it will only be against their former fellows.
You once saved these townsfolk, taught them to suppress desire and maintain purity... yet now you would pardon the very bandits who murdered their friends and family, all for worldly benefit?
Did you imagine the people accepting this?
How is this anything less than a betrayal?
What would you suggest, then?
I would lead our men into the Flat without delay, eliminate the surviving bandits, and destroy the very means by which they have sinned.
Our injured brothers need rest.
We have neither the manpower nor the resources for another major campaign.
I will ensure they are ready.
I hope you'll forgive my bluntness, Señor Alguacil.
But the three bandits in the basement will 'escape' at nightfall. If all goes well, they will soon bring news back to their fellows.
All I need from you is to maintain order when the bandits come to negotiate.
What made you change your mind?
It's just a compass. There wasn't much to think about.
Would someone with so little on their mind have such restless hands? Should I be worrying about you making a hole in my ship, joven?
I'm not that strong.
Come now, don't be like that. I hope you realize that being serious all the time only makes you the butt of the joke.
Now grab a change of clothes. You have blood all over you.
...Gracias.
Come with me.
*Cough* *Cough*...
Sorry, I should have told you to hold your breath. This cabin hasn't been used in decades, and there's dust everywhere.
I'm sorry for touching things without permission.
Don't make me sound like such a petty woman.
You can touch whatever you want here. It's all top-notch alchemical equipment. Unless your hands are more dangerous than cannon fire or a storm, they'll survive.
This will be your lab from now on.
You have a full-fledged alchemy lab on your ship?
Yes.
Before I forget...
These clothes belonged to the previous alchemist. You're about the same size, so try them on.
Oh, uh...
You can change in the storage room to your left.
Do I look weird?
Of course not. It fits you well.
Do you like it? The style is decades-old, but it was tailor-made with the finest fabrics.
It feels brand-new, though.
It was meant to be a coming-of-age gift. But he fell into the sea along with the shells of the Iberian Defensa the very day before reaching adulthood.
I searched for his body myself, but never found a trace. I only hope it was the fins that got him. There weren't quite so many Seaborn back then.
You've fought the Iberian Defensa?
This was before the Profound Silence, when we still sailed the high seas. This very ship, La Conquista, was captured in that era.
I don't suppose you have an interest in history?
No, I've just heard a few tales about the ocean.
Then fix the compass and see it with your own eyes.
First you wanted me to fix your compass, now you want me to follow you out to sea?
It requires an alchemist to operate it.
Let me have a good look at you. Hm... raise your collar a bit, wear the shawl over your shoulder... not bad at all.
Er, I can handle the rest...
Try to avoid any strenuous effort for a few days. Your injuries aren't life-threatening, but given the hygiene on the ship, you can never be too careful.
Yes, yes... Oh! I know what's missing!
How could I forget?
I haven't given you the compass yet.
Isidro takes the compass from Juana and caresses its surface.
It is light, and cold.
I can't guarantee success, but I'll give it my all.
Your all, you say?
When I make a promise, I intend to keep it.
Then you shall have all the support I can offer.
Good. First, I'll need an assistant. I'm not asking a lot, just someone clever and hard-working.
Naturally.
Right, and I have one other present for you.
If what the boss says is true, then we're out of options.
The townsfolk now control the route from the Flat to the inland, and we're their mortal enemies.
There's no way back. We have to face the ocean.
Is there really no other way?
Señor Rus! Señor Javier!
Slow down and breathe. What's going on?
Th-They're back!
Who's back?
O-Our mates who got caught! They escaped and came back!
And they brought someone with them!
Take a look at this scallywag I found outside!
Healthy, works hard, don't eat much. And he's yours for two months of saltfin garum!
His face is kind of nice...
He's all skin and bones. A flick of a saltfin's tail would send him packing...
Hey! I take exception to that!
I'll have you know I'm ripped under this loose fit!
Okay, going for one-and-a-half months of saltfin garum!
For an absolute adonis like me? I'm worth at least a year of that fin stuff!
Shut up already! Can't you see no one wants you?
Going for one year of saltfin garum!
One month!
Two years!
Two weeks!
Three years!
Pah! Three days!
Deal!
Huh?
Three days of saltfin garum, under my name. Deal?
...Huh?
Perfect timing. I was just thinking I needed a bodyguard.
Now what to call you... How about Pelirrojito, for that red streak you've got?
You were hiding all this under the Salt Flat?
Shh, do you want the neighbors to hear?
That wasn't funny.
I'm more surprised you even noticed the joke.
So did you want me to help you kill this Seaborn?
Of course not.
Then why bring me here?
You came to the Salt Flat seeking a school of swordplay founded by a pirate, did you not?
Yes...
Then congratulations, you've found it.
When you need a break from repairing the compass, you can come here and learn from it.
From a Seaborn?
A Seaborn that has devoured countless swordsmen. With every movement of its appendages, it recreates their skills.
Perhaps as an attempt to escape, or perhaps a meaningless act of imitation...
...What exactly is going on here?
It is difficult to explain. After trapping it here, I issued a bounty, inviting any swordsman to try their hand at killing it.
Many have attempted it, but all have become a part of it, along with their blades.
Now I'm even more confused. Why did you trap it here? Why were you looking for someone to kill it?
And why do you want me to learn swordplay from it?
I was never the original captain of the fleet. It was it... him.
Him?
His name was Cruz.
When the waters that inundated this land subsided, the remaining Seaborn began to attack the cities in a frenzy.
We fought hard and slew most of them, but this one was particularly hard to kill. Cruz lured it into a cave, using himself as bait, and both became trapped here.
The last time I saw him, he named me captain and asked me to bring the others back to the sea.
Why not tell them the truth? I've heard the crew spreading terrible rumors about you and the Seaborn.
Let them. A leader needs to be feared as well as loved.
So you came up with this entire plan back then?
No. It was Cruz who gave it to me. He had long expected that he would die before I did, so he told me everything, that I might continue his work after his death.
You should have freed him from... this...
I wanted to. But it's not that easy.
I couldn't do it, not with my own hands... Even though I know it's just a Seaborn. I love him, just as I love the time we spent sailing far and wide.
Promise me. Learn everything you can from it, as much as your little sponge of a brain can absorb.
That way, at least, I'll get to see his swordplay once more.
Leaf? You've got news from Elysium...?
Play recording.
Weedy... I followed the escaping bandits back to their den.
Don't worry, no one knows who I am. And I found someone who can protect me.
She has a lot of connections here. I'm sure I'll find Thorns soon.
Just sit back and wait for the good news.
*Sigh*...
Bring the crates over here, Pelirrojito!
Yes, boss!
Not bad! You're stronger than you look. Now I want to hear that same energy in your selling.
You got it, boss! I've already memorized the entire list of goods, forwards and backwards!
(I'm done listening to you the moment I find Thorns!)
Look, people are coming! Say it so they can hear!
Prosthetic eyeballs! Made from the finest saltfin bones for maximum comfort, in any color you want!
Aged saltfin garum from our special collection! A flavor you won't forget!
Don't miss this chance, señores and señoras!
(Wow... this guy's a natural. Doesn't sound like a slave at all...)
Come one, come all, there's something for everyone!
*Cough* *Cough* I'm getting hoarse, Boss...
Everybody's staring, but why is no one stopping by?
Yeah... it's weirdly quiet. I'd expect someone to have picked a fight with me by now.
Would you care for one, señor? Could come in handy someday!
No thanks, Pascuala...
Well, if you're not buying, you're not buying, but what's that look? Gives me the creeps.
I suggest you put your tail between your legs and behave yourself.
Huh?
Didn't you say you were well-connected?
I am! Just imagine how much worse it'd be for an outsider like you without me here to smooth things over.
(These two never used to give me a second glance. Why'd they stop to glare at me today?)
You know what, just close up the stall and pack up for today. I'm gonna go ask around.
Is it true what those guys were saying?
Yeah, I sent someone to check.
Do you trust them? That if we hand over the compass and the thief, we all get pardoned and can live in town?
It's hard to believe.
Then why'd you let that chaval from the city with the red hair onto our fleet?
I don't know what to believe anymore, Rus.
Could be the fickle sea, could be some inscrutable show of good faith. Whatever it is, keeping him around gives us more options.
If it comes to it... the chica is the easy part: just tie her up and hand her over. But how in the hell would we get the compass from the boss?
I... I'll talk to her. This is about the whole fleet. She'll listen.
Talk?
I used to think maybe you could convince her, but now I'm sure she'd just drag you right back in.
What do you want me to do then?
Who knows? You get to make decisions for the fleet, not me.
(Those Inquisition finshits... Now they're just playing dirty...)
*Sigh*...
What's with all the sighing, boss?
I heard... *sob*... I heard...
Come on, don't cry, you can tell me. Maybe I can help.
You're so nice, Pelirrojito, *sob*...
(Who else would I count on here?)
I'm worried about you.
Ahem. What does this have to do with me, boss?
It's one thing to be drifting around on my own, but now I've got you to care of.
I'm not fired, am I? After all the work I've done for you?
No... I'm just worried about you getting left all alone with these bandits if something happens to me.
(This is practically blackmail!)
...Wow, I didn't think you cared, boss! Don't worry, nothing'll happen to you on my watch!
Oh, Pelirrojito!
Boss!
I... I heard them talking... they want to hand me over to the Inquisition!
The Inquisition chased me all the way into the Salt Flat! All because of a compass that used to belong to my family!
Your family?
I may not look it, but my family used to be rich and famous!
But everything we had was sold off, and the compass somehow ended up with the Inquisition.
Wow, that's amazing, boss!
(Yeah right.)
So, what do you need me to do?
Ahem. I heard the fleet's alchemist is looking for an assistant, and he's got the boss's trust big time. So, uh, maybe you could try applying?
You're clever and hardworking and I'm sure you'll get along like peanut butter and jelly! Then you can put in a good word for me.
Why not just do it yourself?
Well, me and him have a little bit of a history... Maybe more than a bit...
Anyhow, just work that silver tongue keep an eye out for your chance!
No problem, boss! I'll step up as soon as he needs me!
Maybe don't be TOO eager... In the end, it comes down to timing.
We can't make things too easy for him. He's gotta soak in the anxiety, then you can show up right when he needs you most. That way, he'll be helpless without you.
Why are you making this weird?
Both of our fates are in your hands, Pelirrojito.
(You can eat finshit for all I care once I get in that alchemist's good graces!)
Waahh!
H-How could he say that!
Hey, wasn't that Steelface Pele?
Steelface Pele?
Yeah, says he didn't so much as whimper when he fell from the mast last year.
So why was he crying like a little kid after leaving that room?
Are you here to interview for the alchemist assistant's job?
Er... yeah...
Turn back if you know what's good for you, chaval.
Does he have a bad temper?
No, worse.
Don't worry about it, Pelirrojito. He's a nice guy. He even saved my life.
Sure, but work always brings out the worst in us.
Now that you mention it...
He kicked me out in three seconds flat! Said I was breathing too loud!
(Whisper) Hey, boss, you said you had a history with this guy, right?
(Whisper) What about it?
(Whisper) You're trying to throw me into a fire! I'm not going in!
(Whisper) We're already here! You're not out of this that easy!
No, I'm not going! Whatever you're up to, nothing good can come of it!
Just go in! Stop running away!
Get off my shoulders!
No!
Yow! Stop pulling on my eyebrows! I can't see!
Hey, watch where you're going! You're gonna hit the door!
Ow ow ow...
Whatever the reason for this racket, you're interrupting my work.
Either I toss you out the window, or you show yourselves out. Choose.
Sorry, sorry, I just wanted to recommend you an assistant.
You mean another of those guys who couldn't tell a beaker from a flask? No thank you, you can keep them.
You sure don't mince your words, do you?
He's new here, Isidro, doesn't know how things work. Don't mind him.
You...
Yup, me.
Er, excuse me?
Nobody pays any attention to Pascuala. Isidro puts his hand on the shoulder of 'Pelirrojito'.
Who in turn puts his arm on Isidro's shoulder, their two arms linked like cables connecting a bridge.
They pat each other's shoulders so hard, they might as well be punching each other.
Pelirrojito's face turns as red as the streak in his hair, grimacing in pain.
As for Isidro...
Have they been starving you? You pat like a wimp.
Do you even know how much weight I lost looking for you? Hope you're ready to buy me a good meal.
Señores...
How did you find me?
We can talk about that later. Let's talk about you. How did you get here with all your limbs intact, you lucky boy?
...What's with the tone of disappointment?
Excuse me...
Pascuala finds no room in the conversation for her to butt in.
Never mind.
She turns around and leaves, politely closing the door behind her.
That's two arms, two legs, two eyes, two ears, one nose, all present and accounted for. Maybe a bit of a tan?
Thank goodness! You have you no idea how anxious I was when I heard these bandits were stone-cold killers who ate people for breakfast!
And who told you THAT?
Do you know how much courage I had to muster to come here and rescue you? Weedy's still back at town. I'll get in touch with her.
(Whisper) Whenever you're ready, I'll call her, and we can get out of here!
I've tried, it's practically impossible. Besides, I don't plan on leaving for now.
Why not? Don't tell me you actually joined up with these bandits like they say?
(Whisper) Come on, out with it. Or do you really think there's a future out here with these backwater thugs?
...I have no interest in becoming a pirate, Elysium.
I'm staying because... I want to resume the alchemical research I ran from. This compass has Corazonix in it, maybe the last one.
Besides... I promised them I would fix it. It's critical for their survival.
So... you plan to stay with them?
No. Once it's fixed, they'll leave the Salt Flat for the ocean.
Then I'll stay behind and continue to hone my skills in both swordplay and alchemy.
Elysium pats Isidro on the shoulder. His friend seems to have changed.
Sounds like you've had a lot to think about... Well, that's not such a bad thing.
So what exactly did the girl outside want me to do here again?
I needed an assistant as I repair the compass.
Just like back in Rhodes Island, then. Let me know what you need.
Mm-hm.
I will.
Isidro stands before the transmutation table. The probe in his hand makes a sharp sound as it swipes through the air, an Arts energy field forming along the lines that it draws.
He carefully removes the outer casing of the compass and places it on the table. The viscous substance inside begins to liquify, creating waves in the direction of the energy flow.
Arts energy and Corazonix intertwine into a golden light illuminating the salt flat outside through the gaps in the workshop walls.
The sailors have never seen anything like it. They begin to gather one by one.
What are they doing in there?
That kid from outside is doing his thing, that owl-kemmie or whatever... Never though it'd glow gold like that though.
Lemmesee, lemmesee...
Not gonna lie, this is pretty sick. What's that in your hand?
What's that in his hand?
Some beggar's magic trick, what else?
Oh. Well, nothing to see here then. It's not like we have any food to spare.
Bothersome brat...
Come and see my trick! All I ask is a piece of bread—
Ugh...
Get lost, you're in the way.
(Whisper) Bastard...
Er, we're attracting quite a crowd outside. Does that bother you?
Want me to chase them away?
It's fine. With you here chatting away, it feels like I'm right back at Rhodes Island. They won't bother me.
Isidro's hands do not stop. The probe in his hand seems to dance with the Corazonix rippling in the energy field.
His palms emit light, looking to the sailors peeping through the windows like a mirage born of hunger and thirst.
You ever seen anything like it?
Way back when, I remember the boss talking about folks who'd perform tricks for others in the big cities. Guess that's what it looks like?
It's beautiful...
Let them watch. Just make sure they don't barge in and try to sell me for food.
...Sounds weirdly plausible. Do people do that?
People do a lot of things when there's nothing to eat.
Excuse me...
Do you have any food?
No... will you take this ribbon though?
No, it's got to be something I can eat.
I don't have anything to eat myself... I...
I happen to have a few biscuits here. Why don't you let this young lady see your trick?
Biscuits? Sure, since you asked.
Metal begins to flow in young Isidro's palm, glowing with fantastical colors in the darkness.
Passersby cannot help but gather around. The disdain in their words cannot disguise the fact that their eyes are glued to the light in the young boy's hand.
It has been so long since there was something so bright on these streets.
It's so pretty. Can I take a closer look?
Suit yourself.
The boy waves his fingers, and the flowing metal turns into a fowlbeast that takes to the air before returning to his palm in the shape of a fin, then a musbeast, then a burdenbeast...
The metallic burdenbeast runs through the crowd, shiny and chrome, leaving behind stardust in the air.
Wow!
It's just—
Oh, shut up and watch.
When was the last time I saw fireworks...
I think it was a long, long time ago...
Fireworks? What are fireworks?
The child musters the courage to reach out and catch a burst of drifting starlight. She trembles at the warmth upon her fingertips.
Impressive, boy. It's not often that the people here have something to smile about.
Just trying to earn my bread.
Is that so?
Why do you have a smile on your face, then?
The probe guides the Corazonix, drawing a river of stars in the air.
More and more sailors gather, pressing their faces to the window, the thirst, hunger and exhaustion on their faces temporarily replaced by awe and wonder.
Now you're just showing off.
Did you think I couldn't tell?
That was just a check of the alchemy equipment and compass. Now comes the next step: separating the rusted and denatured parts from the Corazonix.
This is what I promised them... what I can do for them.
You two sure spent a long time chatting.
Well, it's been a while since we last saw each other. Were you sitting out here this whole time, boss?
You can stop calling me boss. All my friends call me Peludita, on account of how fluffy I am.
Oh, so we're friends now?
'Course we are! Could you have ever found your old amigo without the help of yours truly?
(Talk about twisting the truth.)
Hm... so what should you call me, now that we're friends?
Anything you like! More importantly, what did Señor Isidro say?
About, you know, putting me under his protection?
Oh, I forgot to ask. But maybe I'll remember tomorrow, if you ask nicely.
Please, o honorable Señor Pelirrojito! The greatest, handsomest Liberi of all!
Ooh, I like that. Keep it coming!
(You flipping finshit!)
(I'm yanking that red bit right out once I'm out of this mess!)
Well, I guess I do owe you one for helping me find my good buddy...
Elysium pats the girl on the head. Her youthful, childlike exterior belies the maturity, even cruelty, lying beneath.
You sure keep some choice company for a girl your age. Well, come find me if you ever squeeze yourself into something you can't squeeze out of.
I'm as kind-hearted as they come, after all!
...Huh?
Hmph! Who'd believe a show-off like you?
At the end of the day, the only one you can trust is yourself.
Another one, please.
That pancake... mm... another plate please!
(Whisper) Have they never eaten in their entire lives?
(Whisper) Isn't it all so very familiar? The pride, the gluttony, the greed.
(Whisper) How are they any different from when they raided your granary?
(Whisper) I don't need them to change.
Ahem... I'm sure you're familiar with Aarón, even if your previous visits have been, shall we say, less peaceful.
Still, I bid you welcome, and look forward to a productive discussion.
*Burp*—
(Raises arm)
Did you need a handkerchief?
I'm good, got my sleeves.
...Very well.
(Whisper) Who decided to serve them fried garlic pancakes?
(Whisper) Do you truly believe you can accept them?
(Whisper) If it benefits the town.
Now that you have eaten, it's time to talk—
Can we get some more of those for take-out?
Dinner is over. It's time to—
Alright, you over there, no need to hide. Come here, and we'll talk.
Yes, you, señor. You're the one calling the shots, right? You can't fool us.
...Negotiation is the calificador's duty. Speak to Señor Silver.
How long have you been waiting here, Javi?
Since morning.
Didn't Teach tell you I had private business I needed to attend to?
In all my time by your side, you've never had 'private business' even once. Do you even have a life outside this fleet?
I never said I did.
So why were you looking for me?
...Would you give up the compass, if we found another way besides going out to sea?
I'm listening.
The mates who got back from Aarón said we'll be pardoned if we hand over the compass and the thief. That they'd let us live in town, or go wherever we want.
And you believe them?
I looked into it. The offer's real, at least.
When? And why wasn't I informed?
Didn't want to bother you while you were recuperating.
...And who asked you to be so protective of me?
How else would I treat the woman who raised me?
It feels like it was only yesterday. Now you're taller than I am.
When I was little, I used to pray to grow up faster, taller, and stronger, so that I could share your burden.
Guess my wish came true.
You'll always be that little boy to me, no matter how big you are.
But what about my thoughts? Will you still treat them as the ideas of a little boy?
No, Javi, I won't. I know it's a feeble concession, the kind that only an adult can make.
But I'd sooner die than surrender to them.
How many of the friends you grew up with have already died at their hands?
I'll never forget, Señora Juana...
My crew and I did everything for them, when times were hardest!
Cruz, myself, and our crew fought the Seaborn to protect them!
We spared what little rations we had, so they wouldn't have to feed on their own corpses!
And how did they repay us? By chasing us into the Salt Flat as soon as the Inquisition arrived!
That never would've happened to Cruz if they hadn't turned a blind eye!
I will never bow my head to those ingrates.
I love you, Javi, all of you, whether you believe it or not. Just as I loved all the mates who fought alongside me amidst the waves.
They're long gone, but I still see them in you all. The legacy of blood works in such mysterious ways.
That's why I can never forgive them... I hope you understand.

eps.name: Let the Light of the Past Die

lines.x: Wow, this is way more dangerous than your experiments at Rhodes Island.
You used to blow up your lab like three or four times a day, at most. Now, you're doing it once every fifteen minutes.
That's not the kind of data I want...
Don't worry, I made detailed records of all your experiments so far. Clean the desk up, won't you, Peludita?
Sure thing!
What is she doing here?
I felt like I could use an assistant myself. Don't worry, she's clever and hardworking enough.
(And you're making me do all your work!)
Ahem. How about you go fetch some fin oil and Oriron from the warehouse?
On it!
Alright, she's gone...
Now, what's the problem we're dealing with?
We're not making much progress... The samples that I've managed to construct are well below the required standard.
Really? They look good though. Nice and shiny.
There's a reason Corazonix is so rare. Its incredible powers are supported by an extraordinarily complex structure.
Either I'm not doing this right, or my understanding of Corazonix is completely wrong.
I'm not ready to manipulate true Corazonix, not even close.
So why not make more samples until you're satisfied with them?
Our resources are limited, I can't use them all... If I fail, I at least want to leave some behind for them.

You've barely started and you're already thinking about your inevitable failure. Come on, have some faith in yourself.
We've worked together so many times. I have faith in you. So does Señora Juana, and, er... er...
...Who else was there again?
Well, that certain someone, at least...
And me.
Oh, hey there, Peludita. You're back already?
I, for one, am rooting for your success.
What's in it for you, Pascuala?
Don't forget I was the one who brought this compass here. If you screw this up, I'm screwed too.
So, don't send me away again when you run into trouble. I'll do what I can to help.
...Well, to start with, you could help by not selling me out this time.
Tch, believe what you want...
Ahem. Anyway, thanks for running that errand. I'm going to adjust the direction of the transmutation table. Could you sort out the ingredients?
Fine, fine.
(All you do is nag!)
Come in!
Sorry to interrupt. There's something I want to ask.

The transmission core on one of the salt ships just broke, and we couldn't find any material strong enough... I get that the boss gave you those materials for the compass, but we just need a little...
How much?
One hundred grams.
Pascuala will fetch it.
(Whisper) Are you kidding me? That's everything we've got!
Go.
But...
Just go. We don't need it for the experiments just yet.
Muchas gracias. Sorry for asking at a time like this. Señora Juana said the compass takes priority over everything else...
The future of the whole fleet is on you.
Isidro?
Please don't say that.
...Is it not going well?
Don't you worry, señora. It's only been a few days! He's feeling stressed, that's all.
If you ask me, it's a good thing! Nothing like a little pressure to keep you sharp.
Word of advice, Isidro. Whatever happens in the lab, good or bad, don't tell a soul.
Resentment towards Juana has been growing for years due to her radical decisions. Many are watching closely, waiting for the slightest slip-up.
Waiting for their chance to take the leadership from her.
I didn't realize there would be so many complications when I agreed to repair the compass.
What about you, Señora Teach?
Are you one of the people watching?
Of course I'm watching you, chaval. But I don't give a sandfin's ass about the compass, I only care about Juana.
If you fail, it's up to me to keep her safe, and...
And what?
And keep you all from being torn to pieces by a horde of angry pirates.
This is a pirate ship, and don't you forget that.
Thorns...
Isidro does not answer. His expression is grave, and the shadows on his face hang like heavy curtains in the night.
Several pirates are still working under the dim lamplight. They look up at the pair with probing gazes as they walk by.
Isidro has never felt so intently that his every move is being watched.
If you're starting to have second thoughts, I've got your back.
(Whisper) I'm sure the two of us could slip out by ourselves, no problem.
...Sorry to drag you into this, Elysium.
Oh, spare me, I chose to come here.
I just wanted to help them find a way out. Maybe satisfy my own desires along the way...
But things are far more complicated than I thought. Far too many people have placed their hopes in me, in this compass.
Some of those hopes are even contradictory. Some want me to succeed, some want me to fail, others want me to do nothing at all.
I don't like it.
I feel you, amigo.
But that's how people are. Anything you do is inevitably going to affect someone else. Not saying you need to feel responsible, but it couldn't hurt to give it some thought.
The whole thing makes my head hurt... I'd much rather be working alone.
You haven't been alone for a long time now, buddy.
You really gave Teach all the sinew?
...Did Pascuala tell you?
Oh, you needn't worry about that. I certainly didn't order her to show up at your lab.
I realize you'd rather not be bothered. I just felt compelled to check on your progress.
Do you need more sinew?

Unless my eyes deceived me, that wasn't saltfin sinew... That was taken from Seaborn.
But I thought you wiped out the Seaborn on the Salt Flat.
Wait... are you going to kill it? But...
...Come with me.
Cruz would have done the same.
I told you Juana wouldn't listen.
I know...
I did it to keep her from learning about your conversation with the shipwrights. She was going to talk to them yesterday.
We simply talked about work. What's there to hide?
Teach may not have realized, but I have. It doesn't take that much sinew to fix a transmission core.
...So you knew all along?
What are your next plans?
The compass repairs will grind to a halt without enough materials.
Juana won't stop just because of a little material shortage. She'll go get more with the kid.
I heard it was taken from the sea monsters Captain Cruz killed... but there aren't any sea monsters around anymore.
No, there are... I've seen it.
So... the rumors were true?
Doesn't matter anymore. What matters is what we can do while she's gone.
Hey, Peludita, have you seen Thorns?
Did he head to the lab already? At this hour?

*yawn*
No clue. He didn't come back last night.
You waited all night for him?
Not me, Nene. It laid down at the door and didn't budge all night.
(Shakes tail anxiously)
That's odd. I'll check out the lab. Go get some sleep.
Can't work without sleep. No point trying to play it tough; I'll wake you up if there's anything.
Did he really think I could fall asleep at a time like this? That guy's loco.
Um, excuse me? Are you looking for someone?
Is Pascuala here?
The sound of clashing swords echoes through the cave. Isidro slices a sharp arc in the air, cutting cleanly through the Seaborn's flesh.
Yet, the creature is relentless, its appendages flailing wildly. Blades embedded within its limbs streak across the air like shooting stars.
A fencer's rapier, a bladecaster's sword-wand, a knight's great sword, an assassin's dagger, a samurai's katana... one after another, they rush towards Isidro, threatening to overwhelm his vision.
Finally, there is a pirate's cutlass, screaming as it cuts through the air, as if battling the wind and waves, like in the legends.
Hmph...
Gugh...
I used to ask myself every day... why wasn't I the one who was devoured?
This was your burden to bear... so why leave it to me?
You were the one who should have sat in the captain's chair, and I should have been your helmswoman, drenched from head to toe by the ocean's spray.
Oh, how I've loathed you for this, Cruz.
The night wind of the Salt Flat carries the familiar smell of brine.
A single tear falls from the corner of Juana's eye, scattered by the wind before it hits the ground.
Your swordplay has already improved tremendously. I thought it would take more time.
I'm sorry...
For what? I'd have joined you in that cave, if I weren't recovering from my injuries.
...Were you crying?
What did I tell you about asking a lady a question she'd rather not answer?
...My apologies.
There's no need to feel bad. I was planning to ask you to kill hi—it eventually, once you were strong enough.
We just moved up the schedule. You must be incredibly talented with a blade to have accomplished it so soon.

It... had a sword with the name Cruz on it.
I thought you should have it.
I... still haven't delivered the killing blow.
You can still say goodbye, if you want.
Gracias.
The Seaborn writhes, its appendages twitching as swords scrape dully against one another.
But this sound soon fades, along with the Seaborn's feeble breathing. Juana knows that the last vestige of its life is slipping away.
With its last bit of strength, it lifts itself from the ground, using the swords impaling it as support.
I'm sorry for imprisoning you in this cave for so long.
Your suffering will end soon.
I'm glad I met that boy, so you could meet your end by the sword in an honorable duel.
Rather than being put down as a monster...
So many have failed... but he succeeded.
If he's willing to go out to sea with us, surely he would be your equal as captain.
Juana approaches the creature, places a hand upon its smooth face, and gazes into its clouded eyes.
It struggles as it senses her approach, but its once-strong appendages betray it, and it falls to the ground.
Juana holds its head and places it upon her knees.
The light-emitting organ on its forehead dims. Juana is reminded of that night many years ago, when she saw a tiny sailboat from beneath the surface.
On that boat stood a young man, gazing curiously into the water, lamp in hand.
May your soul be freed from the Salt Flat, and return to the ocean with the clouds and rain.
May we meet again on the boundless ocean.
Farewell, my love.
Juana feels a stinging pain in her chest. This is good. It is a sign that she still lives, and has not been numbed over the past century.
Deep in the ocean, in an Ægir city, the years pass like a single day.
Deep in the Salt Flat, in an Iberian settlement, life is bland as water.
It is only upon the waves, in strife and struggle, that her blood flows, that her heart beats.
It is only then that she feels hunger, the desire to take a bite out of tomorrow, and cough violently at its taste.
She will bite out the shape of the future, one mouthful at a time, and down it with bitter brine.
Let go of me, you piece of finshit!
Juana and Isidro will have you all killed when they see what you're doing to me!
Shut her up.
Finshit... mmph, nnf!
I've sent out word, Rus. The escort will be ready soon. You leave with the chica.
I'll look for the compass in the boss's room and catch up once I find it.
Er, I'm not quite sure I understand what's going on here. Is this a mutiny?
Could somebody explain things to me?
Do we hand him over too?
Aye.

Thorns won't...
I... mmph, nnf!
Finally, some peace and quiet.
Mmpf...
(Pokes head out from under the table)
(C'mon... over here...)
She tries to signal it with her eyes, but Rus and Javier's footsteps send it back into hiding.
She grits her teeth and opens her eyes wide, trying to put on the fiercest look she can muster to scare it into coming out.
What? Salt got in your eyes?
Hmm?
Just leave her be.
Wish I could, but the way she's scrunching her face is an eyesore.
Mmph?!
Mmph—!!
Hmph, nothing...
Lemme take a look.
As Rus approaches, Pascuala struggles even harder against her fetters.
That is, until she catches a glimpse of Elysium slowly edging towards the table, and the saltfin beneath.
She stops struggling and keeps her gaze firmly on the duo in front of her, doing her utmost not to avert her eyes even a little bit.
Nene? What are you doing here?
Mmph!
What's this on your fin?
That's... Elysium's flag. Something must have happened back at the fleet.
Let's hurry!

eps.name: Let the Light of the Past Die

lines.x: Mmph... mmph!
Mmph—!
Give it up already, will you?
Don't blame me for handing you over to the Inquisition. We all have to look out for ourselves.
I'll take the cloth out of your mouths if you'll quiet down.
(Nods hesitantly)
I'll be quiet...
Yeah, if you know what's good for you.
But even if you hand us and the compass over, that doesn't mean things will end well for you. Ever thought about that?
Yeah...
The bodies of your dead crewmates are still decorating the flagpole outside of town. You sure you won't end up joining them?
No... Not here either...
Where is that blasted compass? The chaval puts it back here every night!
Is this what you're looking for, Javi?
You shouldn't have come back so soon, Juana.
On the contrary, I think I returned at precisely the right time.
Señora, I... I don't have a choice.
Put down the knife, Javi. I taught you how to use it. You don't stand a chance.
You're injured. Might not even be hard.
How did it come to this?
What dissatisfaction could possibly have driven you to point your blade at me?
I was never unhappy with you. I supported every decision you ever made, every idea you ever had.
When my mother left me, you took my hand and said we were family.
Ever since then, I've never been unhappy with you.
Then why?
It's for your good, Juana... for you...
Lies. I'm standing right here.
Tell me! How is it my for own good when you're pointing a blade at me?
The Juana I know wouldn't put the entire fleet in danger for her own selfish desires. She's a brave, wise leader we can trust.
You're not her. You're just a...
Spit it out!
Just a pathetic woman who refuses to face reality, drowning herself in the passions of the past!

I've told you time and again...
The ocean is not our past. It's our future.
That's what you want to believe. No one else in the fleet does. The old generation is dead. The stories they told should've died with them.
Give me the compass, Juana. Don't make me take it.
I'd like to see you try.
Don't you understand?
You really think I made the decision to hand it over to the Inquisition on my own?
A mutiny... So this is what was waiting for me.
What a surprise.
All I want is the compass. All they want is a peaceful future.
Give us what we want, and you can still be our leader. Nothing has to change.
Nothing will change? Don't tell me you actually believe that, Javi?
Stop fooling yourself. At the very least, I wouldn't trust you like before.
You're right.
Sorry to do this to you, boss, but the sea's no place to call home.
We just wanna leave the Flat and live a better life.
Put down your weapons, go back to your tents, and I'll pretend none of this happened.
We're out of options, mates.
We can't keep fooling ourselves.
...Sorry I'm late.
Mmph—! Let go of me!
The hell I will, you finshit eater. I still got the marks from the ropes!
You said nobody would know about this mutiny, Javier!
...Mierda!
I found Señor Rus 'lost' on the Salt Flat, so I brought him back.
What's this about, Javier?
Now now, I wouldn't make any wrong moves if I were you. Not if you still care about your friend here.
Shut up! This is none of your business, outsider!
What do you suggest we do then?
To fight them of course! Burn that blasted town to the ground and take back what's ours!
You're going against the boss over some finshit scheme to kneel before the Inquisition? Did you lose your spine like a gutted fin?
They just killed so many of us and took our supplies, and you can't wait to grovel to them, Javier?
I know Juana taught you better than that.
We're tired, wounded, and can barely muster up half a scrap of food. How're we supposed to fight?
Besides, those Inquisition capullos wouldn't even be after us if that dumb girl didn't bring the compass here.
Er, Señora Juana...?
So just give them the girl! What do you think you're doing, turning yourselves in along with her?
Teach...?
Enough, this isn't helping.
Yeah, why haven't we handed her over yet?
Urk!
Señores and señoras, if I may say something...
You shut your mouth!
Uh...
Cold sweat runs down Pascuala's back as the pirates point their cutlasses at her.
She gulps and tries to wipe her sweaty palms on her pants.
She knows there is only ever one way to get what she wants: fight for it with her own two hands.
Come on, guys, what are you doing to one little girl?
Pelirrojito, you—
So many adults in the room, and the best you can come up with is to hand over a child?
Enough already!
Hey, I'm speaking up for you!
I know! But I can speak for myself!
You folks wanna hand me over? Sure! Why not?
I get why you'd want to beat the finshit out of me, and I'm sure you get why I scammed you.
Violence and deception. That's how we survive. That's all we know.
But you know how they do things in town. Do you really think handing me and the compass over will help you even one bit?
But if you don't, the Inquisition bastards will keep hunting you, and that won't go so well for you either, no?
So what's your point? We're screwed either way?
One week.
Give us one week to fix up that compass!
Yes. I just need a little more time.
If he succeeds, the compass will guide us to the sea, where those finshits will never find us again!
If he doesn't, well, it won't be too late to hand me over and see how that goes!
Not like we could escape with the whole crew watching our every move. What difference could one week make?
In Iberia, you escape one barren hellhole only to end up in another.
Outside the country, your life isn't your own without cold hard cash! It belongs to whoever's paying you!
Señora Juana isn't taking you out to sea just to die. What do you think she's trying to do?
You should know better than me how smart she is. She'd have been way better off leaving your sorry asses behind ages ago.
She could've held your lives in her hands and made you work for pennies, but she didn't.
She just wanted you to be free.
That compass is your ticket to fame and fortune, just like your forefathers! How could you call yourselves pirates, when you've never even seen the sea?
Pascuala feels like her heart is about to leap from her chest. Her clothes are soaked with sweat.
The room is completely silent. She forces a smile and turns to look at Juana, Teach, Isidro, and Elysium behind her, who answer with approving eyes.
Uh... still letting all that sink in?
I got no problem waiting a week. But someone'll be watching you the whole time.
Fine by us.
Anyone else?
I object.
I said, I object!
You're letting a teenage girl make a fool of you like this? You think she knows the first thing about compasses or sailing?
I promise you, if we just get that compass and give it to the Inquisition, then—
Sorry, Javier...
One sailor puts down his weapon, then another, then another. One by one, they walk out of the captain's quarters.
Only Javier and Juana are left, facing off.
I have a mission for you and Rus, Javier. You are not to return to the fleet until it's completed.
Go and pack up. You leave at nightfall.
(Whisper) Let's go, Javier...

lines.st: One week later

lines.x: How's it going?
We've almost reached the deadline...
If, and I mean, IF we fail...
How about you just don't fail!
Señora Juana gave you her full support. Think of everything she's done for this.
Exiling Javier and Rus, two of her most trusted crewmates...
She... she even killed her own...
That's enough, Pascuala.
He knows what needs to be done.
*sigh*...
Stop sighing your heart out.
Today's the day. Aren't you anxious?
What's the use of worrying? Just think about what life would be like if that compass really paid off.
Aye... but what if he fails?
Man, they're really packed around the room. Not even a grain of salt could slip through.
I have a bad feeling about this...
C'mon, don't jinx us like that.
Still...
He's refused any help these last few days, holing himself up in his lab...
He wouldn't let us participate, not even today. Said the experiments require precise control, said that he didn't want to be disturbed.
But if I know anything about him, and I definitely do... he's doing this because he doesn't want to drag anyone else into it.
What do you mean?
Maybe he's preparing to fail.
But the repairs aren't done yet, are they?
That's just how Thorns is. He always expects the worst, and braces himself for it.
What was that?!
The layers of Arts energy field on the transmutation table collapse in an instant. The metallic substance that had gleamed with a shiny glow a moment ago is now a pile of charred mud.
Isidro pulled the probe back just in time to avoid damaging it, but the compass parts he had assembled now lie in pieces.
Isidro runs his fingers through the black mud, trying to figure out the reason for the failure.
But the mud holds no answers. All the clever ideas, the well-laid plans that he had put into it are nowhere to be found.
We're out of time...
Why did I think I could replicate it in such a short time?
I should have known better... master said it took even the most skilled alchemists decades to create Corazonix...
The rusted Corazonix lies still in his pocket, as though mocking his incompetence.
What happened?
She needed no answer. Broken parts litter the ground. The young man sits with his head hanging.
I'm sorry, Juana. I failed.
It's alright, Isidro. This was my own choice.
I'll face the consequences.
It's too risky.
You heard the news. The kid failed. The compass is destroyed.
All the more reason you shouldn't go.
The Inquisition's demands were the compass and blueprints for a salt ship. How're you supposed to parley when we got neither?
Nobody said anything about delivering them personally.
We just need to tell them where to pick them up themselves.
You look like someone who can be reasoned with.
If you mean the mate I sent to parley was too vulgar for you, no need to mince words.
No, what I mean is that you must understand that trust is the basis for all cooperation.
I wouldn't have come myself if I wasn't trustworthy.
And yet you've come empty-handed, asking me to go into the Salt Flat myself to retrieve the bargaining chips you're promising?
How am I to know you're not leading me into an ambush, with a hundred bandits armed to the teeth waiting for me?
Or maybe, I'm offering you more than just the compass and blueprints, but also the location of the pirate base and crewmen who already know how to sail a salt ship?
No, Javier! This was supposed to give us a safe future, not make us their slaves!
Still better than following Juana to a watery death without the compass.
Besides, if you can understand, they can too. They'll cooperate when the Inquisition shows up.
...What about Juana?
If I'm putting more chips on the table, then I need more in return.
Spare Juana.
...You will personally lead the way.
Negotiations are not over yet, Señor Alguacil.
Is that my concern?
...What are you thinking?
He clearly isn't here in good faith. This so-called base of theirs is most certainly a trap.
Let me say this again: We have neither the manpower nor the resources for another major campaign.
My men are in good shape. As for yours... I will ensure they are ready.
I do not doubt your ability, but even if you do defeat the bandits, we cannot afford any more casualties.
We will lack the manpower to build a ship, even if we acquire the blueprints. Aarón will remain trapped in the Salt Flat.
Your negotiations, your deals, none of this is my concern. I have gleaned the location of my enemies, and now I will act.
The ships the bandits have used in their sin shall be put to the torch, along with their blueprints.
As for the sinners... I will exterminate them.
Under whose authority?! The clerics you command grew up in Aarón. We've gone through countless hardships together.
Yet you fail to understand them... Nay, you have betrayed them.
So why do you assume they will listen to you?
(Whisper) I can't believe she'd let us leave... I thought for sure we'd be tied up and handed over to the Inquisition.
(Whisper) Shh...
I know no amount of apologies will make up for your loss.
I suppose this is the end, Isidro.
You needn't worry. I don't blame you. It's not your fault. I accepted the consequences when I made my choice.
I know it was you who bore the brunt of your crew's wrath for us, and I know it was you who saved Pascuala's life.
...Maybe you could come with us.
You would certainly be in grave danger if you stay, señora.
Go. Weren't you discussing how you'd escape earlier? Here's your chance.
I'm the leader of the fleet. I won't abandon my crew. This was my oath to Cruz, and one which I will never turn my back on.
Señora Juana... there's something I need to return to you.
You can keep the clothes and the sword. Consider it payment for your troubles.
No, not that.
What else is there?
The young man reaches into his pocket, but suddenly stops. He lets out a slow breath...
And takes out the object.
The compass? No...
I... I don't understand. If it's undamaged, then what is that debris in the lab?
If you haven't been repairing the compass, then what have you been doing?!
Answer me!
The destroyed compass was a replica. I never touched the original.
Whew...
What?! How come no one ever told me? When'd you make the switch?
I never told anyone.
I don't understand. Why?
The Corazonix in the compass was far more complex than I expected. If I'm unable to replicate it perfectly, that means I haven't understood its true nature.
In which case, attempting to manipulate the actual Corazonix could only lead to failure.
But I couldn't afford to fail. Your hopes, the future of the fleet, the fates of Elysium and Pascuala... all of that was on my shoulders.
So if I was doomed to fail, I thought I should at least limit the damage.
That's why I tried to replicate the Corazonix. If I proved incapable of understanding it, at least you could keep the original and find a better alchemist.
I left some of the materials you gave me behind for the fleet as well.
I'm sorry. I tried.
Isidro... is this your idea of giving it your all?
The chance of failure was at least 87%... This was the safest way I could think of.
Are you that afraid of failure? Even if the chance of success were one in ten thousand, I would have wagered everything on it.
That's not experimentation. That's gambling.
Hah... Have you ever considered that the odds of getting a second chance might be even lower?
Tell me, is life a gamble, or an experiment?
Weedy's on her way to pick me up. Are you coming with us?
...My journey is not yet over.
I will continue.
What about you, Pascuala?
Come with me. I can find a way to get you out of here, at least...
Alright... Take care, Isidro.
Take care.
You too. Both of you.
Señor Silver.
You're willing to stay. Good, very good...
Have you forgotten? I've got a bad leg. Can't go.
...No, I haven't. You were injured during the second raid, correct?
See, you have forgotten. It happened on the day of the execution. You treated the wound yourself, but it ended up infected anyway.
If... hypothetically... if I were a better doctor and fixed your leg then, would you have come with us now?
Of course.
...I see.
What were the two of you discussing? It is time.
Señor Anastasio...
Yes?
It is an honor working with you.
Where were you, señora?
...Saw the kids off.
I did what you told me to.
...Take the crew and Nene. Lie low in the Salt Flat for a few days.
I can't leave you here by yourself.
This is an order, Teach.
Under the moon, Isidro walks by himself through the Salt Flat, leaving behind a trail of lonely footprints.
He does not know how long he has walked. The entire night, probably.
He feels like he has let a lot of people down, but does not know what he did wrong.
Suddenly, he feels lonely and wishes he were with a friend. Then he realizes he just bid farewell to Elysium.
Perhaps Weedy has already found him. Their mission would have come to a close after confirming he was safe.
Perhaps they are discussing the journey out of the Flat and their next mission even now. As for him, he will continue his journey, alone.
Nothing wrong with that. No attachments, no responsibility.
*yawn*
Shouldn't have had those extra swigs of saltfin garum...
Ugh—!
The guards have been eliminated. Inform the Alguacil that the intel is good.
Prepare to advance.
You work fast, Javier.
...You're alone.
As you can see.
You shouldn't have been left unguarded...

Maybe because the one who should have been protecting me is standing before me now.
Pointing the blade I made for him at me.
Go on. Kill me, and the fleet is yours.
Juana is in our custody. Keep an eye on her, but don't let her get hurt.
...What a joke.
How did you find this place...? Which finshit sold us out?!
Even now you think of vengeance? Meet your end, villain!
Gah—!
What of the compass? Have you learned anything?
Nothing, Señor Alguacil. It's all rank-and-file thugs who can hardly string two sentences together.
We won't find anything unless we go deeper into their base.
Do not stop without my direct command. Everything here must be cleansed with fire.
There is no reason to allow such tools of sin to continue to exist.
Sir!
A noise rouses Isidro from his musings and causes him to halt his steps.
He turns around and sees the dark night lit up on the horizon.
That's...
It is the light of a raging fire...
No...
In the direction of the fleet.
How could it...
Isidro steps forward without hesitation.
It is a decision made not with reason, but pure impulse.
He runs as if his life depends on it, retracing his old set of footprints, towards the direction that he came from.
That same set of footprints is no longer lonely.
Don't bother searching. You won't find it.
The sailors told me that the kid screwed up. The compass was wrecked. It'd be of no use even if you found it.
Do not think to presume whether it is of use to me or not, bandit.
Yeah, yeah, perdón.
I have safeguarded the compass for nearly a decade, and slain all who reached for it...
Even if it has become dust, I must see this dust with my own eyes.
Where is your leader?
We had a deal. Juana will be spared.
And how does your agreement with Silver concern me?
Surely the woman fled with the compass, yes? This tale of alchemical failure a ruse?
A lie... to protect a woman, for the sake of your own desires? You, too, ought to be cleansed—
I'm not lying. Juana doesn't have the compass. Whatever's left of it is of no use to her.
It's the alchemist and his lab you should be looking for.
And where might that be?
Second tent in the back row. There's a secret door leading to the lab.
...More lies.
Do not make me cut you down where you stand.
On the ground! Don't move a muscle!
I-Is it true? We'll all get pardoned if you find the compass?
Are we gonna get a lighter punishment if we don't resist?
Fools.
Okay...
Chaval.
Teach? What's going on?
Javier led the Inquisition to us. Señora Juana is being held captive in the captain's quarters.
Why did you come back?
I don't know... Same reason as you, probably.
